channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Market Failure and the need for Stewardship in Early Childhood Services: This is more about oversupply, than a loss of public confidence.
item.description: The announcement of Australia’s second largest provider of early childhood education services G8 Education will close 40 of its services due to key issues such as low occupancy, shortage of experienced educators and financial sustainability, was no surprise to anyone in the sector.
item.content:encoded: The announcement of Australia’s second largest provider of early childhood education services G8 Education will close 40 of its services due to key issues such as low occupancy, shortage of experienced educators and financial sustainability, was no surprise to anyone in the sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Lady Gowrie Queensland appoints Elizabeth Death as Chief Executive Officer
item.description: Lady Gowrie Queensland has announced the appointment of Elizabeth Death as its new Chief Executive Officer, with Ms Death to commence in the role on 18 May 2026.
item.content:encoded: Lady Gowrie Queensland has announced the appointment of Elizabeth Death as its new Chief Executive Officer, with Ms Death to commence in the role on 18 May 2026.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Listening to parents: why helping families recognise quality matters.
item.description: Choosing early learning is one of the most important decisions families make and increasingly, one of the most complex, a new campaign has been launched to help families ask the right questions.
item.content:encoded: Choosing early learning is one of the most important decisions families make and increasingly, one of the most complex, a new campaign has been launched to help families ask the right questions.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Winston Glades Centre Director selected as 2026 Children’s Rights Queensland Ambassador
item.description: Queensland Childcare Services’ (QCCS) Winston Glades Early Education Centre Director Lisa Harris has been selected as a 2026 Children’s Rights Queensland Ambassador, recognising her long-standing commitment to child safety, wellbeing, education and inclusion.
item.content:encoded: Queensland Childcare Services’ (QCCS) Winston Glades Early Education Centre Director Lisa Harris has been selected as a 2026 Children’s Rights Queensland Ambassador, recognising her long-standing commitment to child safety, wellbeing, education and inclusion.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Tasmania opens consultation on major overhaul of teacher registration laws
item.description: The Tasmanian Government has released the draft Teachers Registration Bill 2026 for public consultation, proposing changes to the state’s teacher registration framework designed to strengthen child safety, modernise professional standards and recognise the role of early childhood teachers in early childhood education and care settings.
item.content:encoded: The Tasmanian Government has released the draft Teachers Registration Bill 2026 for public consultation, proposing changes to the state’s teacher registration framework designed to strengthen child safety, modernise professional standards and recognise the role of early childhood teachers in early childhood education and care settings.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Free training to support early childhood educators to become Lifting Literacy Champions - The Sector
item.description: Free training to support early childhood educators to become Lifting Literacy Champions - The Sector
item.content:encoded: Free training to support early childhood educators to become Lifting Literacy Champions - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Moving beyond compliance PD: building practical wellbeing capability in ECEC teams
item.description: A director sits down to plan the annual PD calendar. They look at what's required, book the food safety refresher, lock in the first aid renewal, schedule the child protection training, and tick the boxes. They care deeply about their team and want them to feel supported, both in feeling and in practice. But by the time the compliance requirements are met, there's almost no budget left, and any additional professional development feels more like a chore than an investment.
item.content:encoded: A director sits down to plan the annual PD calendar. They look at what's required, book the food safety refresher, lock in the first aid renewal, schedule the child protection training, and tick the boxes. They care deeply about their team and want them to feel supported, both in feeling and in practice. But by the time the compliance requirements are met, there's almost no budget left, and any additional professional development feels more like a chore than an investment.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 1 May 2026
item.description: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.content:encoded: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.

item.title: National Child and Family Investment Strategy sets clear direction, now governments must deliver

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 17 April 2026
item.description: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.content:encoded: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Fixating on a ‘magic number’ of childcare hours misses what’s most important for kids’ development
item.description: A major Australian study found kids who spend 40 hours or more in childcare may struggle more with social skills and emotions than those who attend for less time.
item.content:encoded: A major Australian study found kids who spend 40 hours or more in childcare may struggle more with social skills and emotions than those who attend for less time.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation
